data.title: Bitcoin supply crunch boosts confidence in $200K target for 2025 — Bitwise CIO
data.description:
Matt Hougan, chief investment officer at Bitwise, predicts Bitcoin (BTC) will reach $200,000 by the end of 2025 due to a supply shock from heightened institutional demand. In an interview with Cointelegraph at Consensus 2025 in Toronto, the executive said that Bitwise's Bitcoin price prediction model is driven exclusively by supply and demand metrics. Hougan laid out the specific figures driving the forecast:"We know that miners will produce 165,000 BTC this year. Already, publicly traded companies have bought more than that
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-supply-crunch-boosts-confidence-200k-target-2025-bitwise-cio?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: DeFi Development plans to work with the BONK community to manage a Solana validation node
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Nasdaq-listed company DeFi Development Corp. announced a partnership with Solana ecosystem Meme coin BONK and will jointly manage a Solana verification node with BONK. This is the first time that a Meme coin community has shared Solana staking infrastructure with a listed company. The cooperation will also extend to the integration of BONK's own liquidity staking token BONKSOL. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Chainlink Labs Launches Build on Solana Startup Support Program
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Chainlink announced on the X platform that Chainlink Labs has launched a startup support program "Build on Solana" to help teams build, start and grow in the Solana ecosystem. Applications are now open. Selected teams will join Chainlink Build and receive practical guidance, ecological incentives, and marketing resources from the Chainlink and Solana Foundations.

data.title: Federal Reserve Plans 10% Workforce Reduction in Coming Years
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market reports indicate that the Federal Reserve is planning to reduce its workforce by approximately 10% over the next few years. This decision comes as part of a broader strategy to streamline operations and improve efficiency within the organization. The move is expected to impact various departments, although specific details on the implementation timeline and affected areas have not been disclosed. The Federal Reserve's decision reflects ongoing efforts to adapt to changing economic conditions and technological advancements.

data.title: Market Shows Divergence in Funding Rates Across Major Exchanges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass indicates that funding rates across major centralized and decentralized exchanges are showing a mix of bearish and neutral sentiments. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging this fee, to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring contract prices remain close to the asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% signifies a baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, a funding rate below 0.005% suggests a bearish market outlook.

data.title: Ethereum Co-founder: ETH is not a competitor to BTC, but its market value will eventually surpass Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Ethereum co-founder Anthony Di Iorio said at the 2025 Consensus Conference that Ethereum aims to be a substitute for Bitcoin, not a competitor. Di Iorio is one of the eight co-founders of the Ethereum blockchain, but he initially entered the cryptocurrency field by advocating Bitcoin, and believes that Ethereum may eventually surpass Bitcoin in market value. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Market News: Trump tax bill fails in House subcommittee
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market news: Due to differences among the US Republicans on cost issues, Trump's tax bill failed to pass in the House of Representatives subcommittee. Hard-line US conservatives opposed the bill and sought further cuts to Medicaid. (Jinshi)
